questions.question: Create a user 'user1' in the database and grant all permissions (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) to the user 'user1'. Provide the SQL commands to create the user and grant the permissions.
questions.hint: Example: CREATE USER 'user1'@'%' IDENTIFIED BY 'password'; GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON *.* TO 'user1'@'%';

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES' statement in SQL and how does it differ from 'GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE'?
questions.hint: Example: GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON *.* TO 'user1'@'%' grants all privileges, whereas GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE grants only specific privileges.

questions.question: Explain the difference between the '@%' and '@localhost' syntax in the 'CREATE USER' and 'GRANT' statements. Provide an example.
questions.hint: Example: CREATE USER 'user1'@'%' IDENTIFIED BY 'password'; GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON *.* TO 'user1'@'%' grants permissions for all hosts, whereas CREATE USER 'user1'@'localhost' IDENTIFIED BY 'password'; GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON *.* TO 'user1'@'localhost' grants permissions only for localhost.
